code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test15-NOV-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance and color, suggesting possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy), though HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease.
